description: E2E test case designs for opencode-server crate - HTTP server and REST API

test_cases.title: Server startup and health endpoint
test_cases.description: Test server starts and health check responds

test_cases.title: Session CRUD via REST API
test_cases.description: Test create, read, list, delete session endpoints

test_cases.title: WebSocket session hub messaging
test_cases.description: Test WebSocket connection and message broadcasting
expected_outcomes: Messages broadcast to all subscribers in session

test_cases.title: Config endpoint get and patch
test_cases.description: Test config GET and PATCH endpoints
steps: PATCH with invalid field - verify error response

test_cases.title: Provider API endpoints
test_cases.description: Test provider CRUD endpoints

test_cases.title: CORS middleware configuration
test_cases.description: Test CORS headers on requests

test_cases.title: Share session endpoint
test_cases.description: Test share link generation

test_cases.title: mDNS service discovery
test_cases.description: Test mDNS advertisement and discovery

test_cases.title: Session locking across REST and WebSocket
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Verify session lock blocks REST modifications during agent execution via WebSocket
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data corruption from concurrent REST and WebSocket session access
test_cases.preconditions: Server running, session with ID exists
steps: Start long-running agent via WebSocket (send message that triggers agent)
While agent running (session locked), attempt DELETE /api/sessions/{id} via REST
Verify REST call returns 423 Locked or 409 Conflict
expected_outcomes: Session lock prevents concurrent REST modifications
Error response clearly indicates session is locked
Lock held until WebSocket closes or agent completes
post_checks: After WebSocket closes, REST delete succeeds
Session can be deleted normally when not locked

test_cases.title: Concurrent session creates with same IDempotency
test_cases.description: Concurrent POST /api/sessions creating sessions with same idempotency key
steps: Immediately send same POST again with same Idempotency-Key
Verify exactly one session created OR second returns existing session

test_cases.title: WebSocket reconnection with missed message recovery
test_cases.description: Client reconnects after disconnect and receives missed messages
steps: Server continues, 3 more messages sent by agent
expected_outcomes: Client receives messages sent during disconnection

test_cases.title: Request timeout and connection close mid-request
test_cases.description: Server handles slow requests and client disconnects gracefully
test_cases.risk_addressed: Hung connections, resource leaks on client disconnect
steps: Send request to endpoint that takes 10s to process
Verify server can still accept new requests

test_cases.title: Large payload rejection
test_cases.description: Server rejects requests with body exceeding size limit
test_cases.risk_addressed: Memory exhaustion via large request body
test_cases.preconditions: Server running, max body size configured
steps: Send POST /api/sessions with body of specified size
Verify response code appropriate for size
expected_outcomes: Exceeds limit: 413 Payload Too Large or similar
No server crash or OOM on huge payload

test_cases.title: Malformed JSON in request body
test_cases.description: Server rejects malformed JSON gracefully
test_cases.risk_addressed: Crash or unexpected behavior on malformed input
steps: For each malformed body, POST to /api/sessions
Verify server does not crash or hang

test_cases.title: Server graceful shutdown during active connections
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Server must drain connections gracefully on shutdown
test_cases.risk_addressed: Data loss or client error during server shutdown
test_cases.preconditions: Server running with active WebSocket clients
steps: Begin long-running agent operation via WebSocket
Verify server waits for active operations to complete
After timeout, force shutdown if still running
post_checks: Server exits cleanly (exit code 0 or SIGTERM handled)

test_cases.title: Port reuse after crash or unexpected exit
test_cases.description: Server must handle port still in TIME_WAIT after crash
test_cases.risk_addressed: Server unable to restart on same port after crash
test_cases.preconditions: Server was previously running on port 8080 and crashed
steps: Verify port 8080 is in TIME_WAIT state (netstat or ss)
expected_outcomes: Server binds to port despite TIME_WAIT

test_cases.title: Share token forgery prevention
test_cases.description: CRITICAL: Forged share tokens must be rejected
test_cases.risk_addressed: Unauthorized access to shared sessions via token guessing
test_cases.preconditions: Server running, share link generated for session
steps: Generate share link for session A
Create forged token (random string, modify valid token)
expected_outcomes: No information leakage about valid tokens

test_cases.title: Share token reuse after expiry
test_cases.description: Expired share tokens must not grant access
steps: Create session with share link with TTL=1 second
Wait for token to expire (2+ seconds)
expected_outcomes: Error message does not reveal token existed

test_cases.title: CORS wildcard credentials conflict
test_cases.description: CORS must reject wildcard Origin with credentials
test_cases.risk_addressed: Misconfigured CORS allowing credentialed requests to any origin
steps: Configure CORS with Access-Control-Allow-Origin: * and Allow-Credentials: true
Verify request is rejected OR origin is validated
expected_outcomes: Wildcard + credentials combination rejected at config level OR
Credentials not sent to untrusted origins

test_cases.title: Invalid session ID format in API requests
test_cases.description: API must validate session ID format and reject malformed IDs
test_cases.risk_addressed: Panic or unexpected behavior on malformed input
steps: For each malformed ID, call GET /api/sessions/{id}
Verify server does not crash or panic
expected_outcomes: Malformed ID rejected with 400 Bad Request
No path traversal possible via ID

critical_invariants: Session lock blocks concurrent REST access
high_risk_scenarios: server_conc_001: Session lock race across REST/WS
